name: Al-Udeid Air Base
description: Largest US air base in Middle East. CENTCOM forward HQ and Combined Air Operations Center.
strikeDetails: Targeted by Iranian missiles Feb 28. On Mar 4, two Iranian Su-24s intercepted and shot down by Qatari F-15s before reaching the base.

name: Camp Arifjan
description: Primary US Army base in Kuwait. Forward HQ for US Army Central (ARCENT) and Third Army.
strikeDetails: Targeted by Iranian missiles Feb 28-Mar 1 during Operation True Promise IV. Damage reported but base remained operational.

name: Camp Buehring
description: US Army training and staging base in northern Kuwait. Key transit point for troop deployments.
strikeDetails: Struck during Iranian strikes on all US Gulf bases beginning Feb 28.

name: Naval Support Activity Bahrain
description: HQ of US Naval Forces Central Command (NAVCENT) and US Fifth Fleet.
strikeDetails: Targeted by Iranian missiles and drones Feb 28-Mar 1. Iranian drones also struck nearby Bahrain infrastructure on Mar 3.

name: Al Dhafra Air Base
description: US Air Force base near Abu Dhabi hosting fighters, surveillance platforms, and refueling tankers.
strikeDetails: Targeted during Iran's massive strike on UAE Mar 2 involving 165 ballistic missiles, 2 cruise missiles, and 541 drones.

name: Camp Lemonnier
description: Only permanent US military base in Africa. Supports Horn of Africa and Arabian Peninsula operations.

name: Al-Asad Air Base
description: Large air base in Anbar Province, western Iraq. Previously struck by Iranian missiles in Jan 2020.
strikeDetails: Targeted by Iraqi militia operations (Islamic Resistance in Iraq, Kataib Hezbollah) with missiles and drones from Mar 2.

name: Erbil Air Base
description: US military facility at Erbil International Airport in Kurdistan Region. Hosts special operations and intelligence units.
strikeDetails: Targeted by Iraqi militia missiles and drones on Mar 2 as part of 28 claimed operations against US forces.

name: Tower 22
description: Small US Army outpost near al-Tanf at the Syria-Jordan-Iraq tri-border area. Supports counter-ISIS operations.

name: Camp Victory / Union III
description: US military compound in Baghdad's International Zone (Green Zone). Houses diplomatic and military advisory elements.
strikeDetails: Targeted by Iraqi militia operations with missiles and drones on Mar 2 as part of 28 claimed operations against US bases.

name: Incirlik Air Base
description: Major NATO and US Air Force base in southern Turkey. Hosts approximately 50 US B61 nuclear gravity bombs. NATO air defenses intercepted 3rd Iranian ballistic missile over nearby Adana airspace on Mar 13, 2026.
strikeDetails: Not struck directly — NATO intercepted Iranian ballistic missile over Adana airspace nearby on Mar 13, 2026. Residents woken by sirens at 3:25am.

name: Naval Support Facility Diego Garcia
description: Remote Indian Ocean atoll base. Hosts B-2 and B-52 bombers used for long-range strikes into the Middle East.

name: Port Shuaiba
description: US Naval logistics port in Kuwait. Critical maritime logistics node for US forces in the Gulf.
strikeDetails: Struck by Iranian missiles Mar 1, 2026 — 6 US service members killed, 18 wounded. Highest single US military casualty event of the conflict.

name: Makhmour Coalition Base
description: Coalition military base near Makhmour in Erbil province, Iraq. Hosts multinational forces including French troops supporting anti-ISIS operations.
strikeDetails: Drone strike by Iran-backed Iraqi militias on Mar 12, 2026 — 1 French soldier killed, several others wounded. France's first combat fatality in the Iran-Israel conflict.
